item.title: How to delete your account | OpenAI Help Center
item.description: Delete your account by submitting a request to our Privacy Portal or within ChatGPT directly.

item.title: How do I cancel my ChatGPT subscription? | OpenAI Help Center
item.description: Subscription cancellation options and details

item.title: Statement on the comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth
item.description: Anthropic's response to the Secretary of War and advice for customers

item.title: Managing Netplan with Django in Production (Revisisted)
We really do need to get money moving differently. – Wilsons Blog
item.description: A simple model of how the economy works is it’s some kind of rewards system. You do something good that other people will pay you for and you are then rewarded with money. You are a smart engineer and your boss gives you a raise, you’re a smart entrepreneur and VCs give you tons of money. That money entitles you to stuff, the idea being that there is only so much stuff, yachts, housing, fancy clothes, holidays, and you are rewarded for being good at something by being first in line to get that stuff in lieu of someone else getting it. And then we say that this encourages people to work hard and be smart, and that is good for society overall. This is perhaps true to a degree but it is in many ways the less interesting (and less important) part of what “the economy” is.

item.title: I Was Tired of Juggling My Agents, So I Hired a Middle Manager
item.description: I built a persistent manager agent to orchestrate coding workers, handle merge logistics, and keep me out of the context-switching grind.
item.author: (Sawyer Hood)

item.title: Please, please, please stop using passkeys for encrypting user data
item.description: Passkeys are the future of authentication, but using them for data encryption is a disaster waiting to happen. Overloading these credentials creates a dangerous blast radius that can lead to the irreversible loss of a user's most sacred memories and documents.

item.title: Zugunruhe | Nathan's Notes
item.description: Ideology, beyond robustness and agency

item.title: Filesystems are having a moment
item.description: a digital garden by daniel phiri

item.title: LLMs Out of Context
item.description: Context engineering, context rot, and how LLMs navigate content far exceeding their context window.

item.title: There is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide

item.title: My Fully Automated Labor Law Research Tool Is Finally Here – Matt Bruenig Dot Com
The thieves are upset about theft — Cyrus Radfar
item.description: AI labs trained on everyone's data without asking. Now they're furious that other AI labs are training on their outputs. The pattern is older than electricity.

item.title: When DNS Load Balancing Is Good Enough — and When It Will Quietly Fail You
item.description: DNS round-robin works fine until your fleet becomes dynamic, your services multiply, or your datacenters need to coordinate. Here is where the line is, and what breaks when you cross it.

item.title: memory is the residual of surprise
item.description: what neurobiology knows about memory that AI agents forgot. on prediction error, bi-temporal modeling, and why most memory systems are junk drawers.
item.author: (brgsk)

item.title: We Will Not Be Divided
item.description: Employees of Google and OpenAI stand together to refuse the Department of War's demands to use AI models for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous killing without human oversight.

item.title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of External Feedback Loops
item.description: If the agent can't be forced to confront ground truth, you didn't build a software factory - you built a demo generator.

item.title: Developers Are Safe

item.title: Some notes on unreliability of LLM APIs
item.description: Because my book, LLMs for Mortals, was created with Quarto, it runs the code when I compile the book. It uses cached versions when no code changes, but it is guaranteed to be working code for the p…

item.title: Even with a Fleet of Coding Agents, You Still Can’t Recreate StackOverflow in a Weekend
item.description: February 27, 2026

item.title: Hello burnout my old friend... - Trisha Gee
item.description: I am struggling. There. I said it. Yes, I'm middle-aged. Yes, I have kids. Yes, I have an aging parent who has health issues. Yes, I'm building a house. (shhhhhh: yes I'm perimenopausal). But it's not just that (just! Ha!). I want to help people. I LIKE helping people. I like learning. I like trying … Continue reading "Hello burnout my old friend…"

item.title: LLMs don't suffer · honnibal.dev
item.description: The "LLM welfare" question provokes a lot of uncertainty in people, but it really shouldn't. The analogy between LLM computations and what we view as morally relevant experiences is very loose.

item.title: Leaving Google has actively improved my life

item.title: We Need Process, But Process Gets in the Way
item.description: How do you manage a company with 50,000 employees? You need processes that give you visibility and control across every function such as technology, logistics, operations, and more. But the moment you

item.title: Dental Formulas
We deserve a better streams API for JavaScript
item.description: The Web streams API has become ubiquitous in JavaScript runtimes but was designed for a different era. Here's what a modern streaming API could (should?) look like.
